placeholder_sectors.ckm_matrix: trivial identity at kappa_0; mixing angles = 0
placeholder_sectors.cosmological_constant: 56.5 orders-of-magnitude too large at tree level; well-known SM problem; requires loop corrections (Stage 7)
placeholder_sectors.yukawa_charged_lepton: deferred; e^c modes = 0 in degree-1 H^1 rank-4 path; requires higher-degree H^1 exploration
placeholder_sectors.yukawa_up_quark: kappa_0; values dimensionally inconsistent (~TeV vs PDG ~GeV-scale m_t); requires Ext^1 SU(4) extension data from BHOP §6.5 (not numerically published)

next_steps: Engage off-diagonal Ext^1 in BHOP-MonadAdapter (currently kappa=0)
Lift to higher-degree H^1 for e^c, nu^c sectors

sectors.name: kinetic
sectors.description: Canonical fermion + Higgs kinetic terms. Three generations of SM matter (Q_i, u^c_i, d^c_i, L_i, e^c_i, nu^c_i; 12 chiral fields) come from H^1(X̃/Γ, V_BHOP); the κ=0 catalogue populates Q/Uc/Dc/L (12 modes) and defers Ec/NuC. The Higgs doublet H comes from a 10 of SO(10), Wilson-broken to (1,2,1/2).
sectors.notes: Coefficients are 1 by canonical normalisation choice (kinetic-normalised basis). The 4D R/c^2_4 = V_X / kappa^2_10 reduction is reported in the gravity-sector block under provenance.newton_check.

sectors.name: gauge
sectors.symbol: L_gauge = -1/4 sum_a F^a_{munu} F^{a,munu}, a in {SU(3)_C, SU(2)_L, U(1)_Y, U(1)_X}
sectors.description: SM gauge kinetic terms. Coefficient = V_X / (4 alpha' kappa^2_10) times the BHOP-2005 §6 trace-decomposition factor for each group. The structure-group SU(4) ⊂ E_8 has commutant SO(10); Wilson-line breaking by Z_3 × Z_3 reduces SO(10) → SU(3)_C × SU(2)_L × U(1)_Y × U(1)_X with U(1)_X breaking via Green-Schwarz Stueckelberg (substrate paper Step 0.6). Numerical g_1, g_2, g_3 at M_Z are taken from PDG 2024 (GUT-normalized convention g_1^2 = 5/3 g'^2).
sectors.notes: Structural prediction is the ratio g^2_a : g^2_b at the unification scale via the SO(10) → SM trace decomposition (committed pre-Stage-3); the absolute scale is set by V_X / kappa^2_10. The numerical values reported are PDG 2024 anchors at M_Z.

sectors.name: yukawa
sectors.symbol: L_Yukawa = -Y^u_{ij} \bar{Q}_i u^c_j H - Y^d_{ij} \bar{Q}_i d^c_j \tilde{H} - Y^e_{ij} \bar{L}_i e^c_j \tilde{H} - Y^nu_{ij} \bar{L}_i nu^c_j H + h.c.
sectors.description: κ=0 PLACEHOLDER Yukawa matrices from frame-collapse overlaps on the rank-4 SU(4) BHOP shadow bundle. Y_u and Y_d are diagonal in this regime (no Ext^1 deformation engaged). Y_e and Y_nu are zero because the e^c and nu^c slots carry zero modes in H^1(X̃/Γ, V) at degree 1 of the rank-4 shadow (BHOP §6.3). Up-quark mass eigenvalues (descending GeV): [1668.01705838181, 1603.1896971389176, 1556.0535766990115]. Down-quark mass eigenvalues (descending GeV): [1668.01705838181, 1603.1896971389176, 1556.0535766990115]. Both sets are dimensionally inconsistent vs PDG (m_t ≈ 173 GeV) — this is expected at κ=0 and reflects the engineered frame-collapse convention, not a derived prediction. CKM matrix is the 3×3 identity at κ=0; mixing angles all vanish.

deferred.Y_e: e^c modes deferred to higher-degree H¹ via the M3 lift's BHOP §6.3 classification — Ec count is 0 at degree 1 of the rank-4 SU(4) shadow.
deferred.Y_nu: ν^c modes deferred to higher-degree H¹ via the M3 lift's BHOP §6.3 classification — NuC count is 0 at degree 1 of the rank-4 SU(4) shadow.

sectors.notes: DO NOT treat these Yukawa values as physics predictions. They are a structurally-honest κ=0 placeholder pending engagement of off-diagonal Ext^1 in BHOP-MonadAdapter. The Y_e, Y_nu zero entries are NOT a prediction of massless leptons — they are a 'no eigenmode in this slot at this cohomology degree' sentinel, deferred to higher-degree H^1 exploration.

sectors.name: higgs
sectors.description: SM Higgs potential with structurally-derived coefficients anchored to the framework's m_H (chain position k_H = 1072/30) and v_C (Eq. v-ladder) predictions. NO Stage 3 numerics are required. m_H_predicted = 125.199781354230815106095591677 GeV (PDG 2024 central is 125.20 GeV). v_C_predicted = 246.219650019852561793722730693 GeV (PDG tree v = 246.21965 GeV).
sectors.notes: Tree-level SM normalisation: minimum at |H|^2 = mu^2 / (2 lambda) = v^2 / 2; m_H^2 = 2 mu^2 = 2 lambda v^2. The structural prediction matches PDG 2024 m_H = 125.20 ± 0.11 GeV inside the 1-sigma bar.

sectors.name: cosmological_constant
sectors.description: Tree-level Higgs vacuum energy at the broken minimum: V_min = -m_H^2 v^2 / 8 = -118785265.630006015228464140905 GeV^4. In M_Pl_reduced^4 units this is -3.37716825384662386119268026967e-66, vs Planck 2018 Lambda_obs = 1.1e-122 M_Pl^4. The ratio is 56.48716 orders of magnitude — the well-known SM cosmological-constant problem at tree level. NB: V_min is NEGATIVE (Higgs broken-minimum) while Lambda_obs is POSITIVE (de Sitter); the framework's Stage 7 task is to compute the radiatively-corrected vacuum energy including gauge-boson + fermion + scalar loops.

coefficients.sign_note: tree-level Higgs V_min is NEGATIVE; observed Lambda is POSITIVE

sectors.notes: DO NOT treat the tree-level value as the framework's final prediction. Loop corrections (Stage 7) are required. The cosmological-constant problem is acknowledged here, not solved.

newton_check.F_e_gateway_consistent: YES: m_Planck(F_e) / m_Planck(G_CODATA) matches to -1.85963e-6 relative.

stage4_python_geometry.bundle: Rank-4 SU(4) BHOP-2005 Sec. 6 extension; structure group SU(4) subset E_8; commutant SO(10); Wilson-line break Z_3 x Z_3 -> SU(3)_C x SU(2)_L x U(1)_Y x U(1)_X (U(1)_X via Green-Schwarz Stueckelberg)
stage4_python_geometry.manifold: Schoen Calabi-Yau quotient X / (Z_3 x Z_3)
